title: Dockerfile not found when passing context through stdin to podman-remote
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

It seems tarred context passed through stdin to `podman-remote build` does not work, while it does work with `podman build`. It keeps failing with an error saying that the Dockerfile was not found, e.g. :
```
Error: stat /var/tmp/libpod_builder983038179/build/tmp/test/Dockerfile: no such file or directory
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run the API on an isolated host/container: `podman system service -t 0 tcp:0.0.0.0:8080`
2. On another isolated host/container, create a Dockerfile, e.g.:
```Dockerfile
FROM docker.io/ubuntu
RUN echo test
```
3. Tar it: `tar -cvzf ./context.tar.gz Dockerfile`
4. Build the docker image: `podman-remote --url tcp://podman:8080 build -f Dockerfile - < context.tar.gz`

**Describe the results you received:**

That last command fails with:
```
Error: stat /var/tmp/libpod_builder042876891/build/tmp/test/Dockerfile: no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Following the exact same steps with a non-remote podman builds the image correctly:
```
$ podman build -f Dockerfile - < context.tar.gz
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/ubuntu
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5d3b2c2d21bb done
Copying blob 75adf526d75b done
Copying blob 3fc2062ea667 done
Copying config 4dd97cefde done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: RUN echo test
test
93ab8573452867fe442a4d4f806afe92e43907496fb28a3858e23fc1a80a0b49
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

On the process running the API, this is the only logs I got:
```
time="2021-03-08T16:48:55Z" level=info msg="APIHandler(0d1221c7-099c-40df-aeac-b21b068a5b69) -- POST /v2.0.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=%2Ftmp%2Ftest%2FDockerfile&forcerm=1&isolation=1&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&output=tarred%3Acontext&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&rm=1&shmsize=67108864&t=tarred%3Acontext BEGIN"
time="2021-03-08T16:48:55Z" level=debug msg="APIHandler(0d1221c7-099c-40df-aeac-b21b068a5b69) -- Header: X-Registry-Config=<hidden>"
time="2021-03-08T16:48:55Z" level=debug msg="APIHandler(0d1221c7-099c-40df-aeac-b21b068a5b69) -- Header: User-Agent=[Go-http-client/1.1]"
time="2021-03-08T16:48:55Z" level=error msg="unable to write build event: \"write unixgram @055c5->/run/systemd/journal/socket: sendmsg: no such file or directory\""
time="2021-03-08T16:48:55Z" level=debug msg="APIHandler(0d1221c7-099c-40df-aeac-b21b068a5b69) -- POST /v2.0.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=%2Ftmp%2Ftest%2FDockerfile&forcerm=1&isolation=1&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&output=tarred%3Acontext&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&rm=1&shmsize=67108864&t=tarred%3Acontext END"
```

I see that the dockerfile parameter in that request is: `/tmp/test/Dockerfile` (`/tmp/test` being the place from where I ran the `podman-remote` command). Could it be related to this?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman-remote info --debug
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.19.4
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.26, commit: '
  cpus: 9
  distribution:

    version: "20.04"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: podman-0
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 10000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000

    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 10000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 256000
  kernel: 4.19.114
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 2861637632
  memTotal: 20790378496
  ociRuntime:
    name: /usr/bin/crun
    package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun'
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.18.1-7931a-dirty
      commit: 7931a1eab0590eff4041c1f74e2844b297c31cea
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    selinuxEnabled: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.8
      commit: unknown
      libslirp: 4.3.1-git
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.4.3
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 314h 24m 7.86s (Approximately 13.08 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /home/agent/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: 'fuse-overlayfs: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs'
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.9.0
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.4
        FUSE library version 3.9.0
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /home/agent/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 5
  runRoot: /tmp/podman-run-10000/containers
  volumePath: /home/agent/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.0.0
  Built: 0

  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.0.1

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 100:3.0.1-2 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 100:3.0.1-2 arm64
podman/unknown 100:3.0.1-2 armhf
podman/unknown 100:3.0.1-2 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This is working for me in the main branch now.
